title: Grevillea neodissecta
taxonomy: Grevillea neodissecta is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It belongs to the family Proteaceae. The genus Grevillea is a group of flowering plants native to Australia, and it is named after Charles Robert Hugues Greville, a British naturalist and patron of botany. Grevillea neodissecta is classified as a shrub, and its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Magnoliophyta. It is a part of the order Proteales and the subclass Rosidae.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as wounds, infections, and skin irritations.
